# Whhite

> A catalog of 40+ tools and experiments built by Ibrahim Mokdad and Yazin Alirhayim, deployed at *.whhite.com.

Whhite is a collection of independently deployable software projects covering five categories: Claude Ecosystem tools, Developer Tools, AI Apps, Islamic tools, and Life & Consumer apps. Every project is built by the same two-person team in Bahrain and shipped as a live product.

The Claude Ecosystem projects (claudemonitor, claudeview/Claude Garden, claudecost, taskaude) all monitor or visualize Claude Code AI coding sessions in different ways — live dashboards, plant metaphors, cost tracking, and task inspection.

The Developer Tools (disksizer, winopenoats, recoverer, mdpdf, svger, figma-to-pr, saas-cloner, trading-guardrails) are standalone utilities for Windows developers, with an emphasis on local execution, speed, and zero bloat.

The AI Apps (postwriter, doc-ai, tabib, translatebh, style-trainer, rep-counter, reflect-app, gender-pitch-demo, bookwise, cold-cannon, detectcost) are AI-powered consumer and professional tools, many with a focus on privacy and local processing.

The Islamic tools (learnislam, noor, inheritance-calc/faraid, mirath, zakatfitr, quran-tracker, offline-tarteel, sujoodsaho) serve the Muslim community with scholarship-accurate calculators, educational games, and prayer guides.

The Life & Consumer apps (firstbite, sitternow, clustercal, say-it-first, chicken-empire, talabat-optimizer, racegame, resist-garden, voice-quest) are regional consumer products for the GCC market.

## The Makers

Ibrahim Mokdad — engineer and ML specialist, MSc with distinction, YouTube educator (74K+ views on ML tutorials), co-founder of EXA.io.

Yazin Alirhayim — founder (YC W22), former payments company founder acquired by Amazon, Six Sigma Black Belt, has shipped 100+ AI prototypes.

Together they run The Clear Hour, an AI workflow consultancy helping professionals save 5+ hours per week through AI tools and process optimization.
